title: Olotrema acuminata
uses: Olotrema acuminata, also known as the yellow stinkwood, has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices, particularly in Africa. Its bark and roots are said to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could be beneficial in treating infections and wounds. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and safety. Traditional healers in Africa continue to use Olotrema acuminata in their practices, highlighting its significance in local medicine.
